title: Friday Sermon 5 July 2002
description:
Allah is the Light of the heavens and the Earth.  The similitude of His light is as a niche, wherein is a lamp.  The lamp is in a crystal globe.  The globe is bright as it were a glittering star lamp lit from a blessed olive tree neither of the east nor of the west, whose oil would well-nigh glow forth even though fire touched it not.  Light upon light!  Allah guides to His light whosoever He wills.  And Allah sets forth parables for people, and Allah knows all things completely.

('The Holy Quran'.  Al Noor [The Light]: 36).

It is narrated on the authority of Abu Dharr: I asked the Messenger of Allah: Did you see thy Lord?  He replied ‘He is Light; how could I see Him?’

(Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj.  'Sahih Muslim').

Narrated Abu Saeed al-Khudri: The Messenger of Allah said ‘Beware of the believer's  intuition, for indeed he sees with Allah's Light.’  Then he recited: Surely in this are signs for the intelligent (Al Hijr [The Stoneland]: 76).

(Abu Isa Muhammad ibn Isa al-Tirmidhi.  'Jami al-Tirmidhi').
 
Allah’s Light also connotes the Divine Order through which the heavens are illuminated by the angels, the earth is illuminated by the Prophets of God as indeed are the physical universe with the heavenly bodies that the sun and the moon. 
  
Allah the Exalted governs the heavens and the earth with illuminating rationale and that Light also connotes guidance and Allah is indeed the Guide for all.
  
Furthermore  the quality of a believer to be rightly guided is like a light he also said that the clarity of rational alone does not bring about guidance and advantage, rather it only comes with Allah’s grace.

(Fakhruddin al-Razi.  Tafsir e Kabir: 'Mafatih al-Ghayb').

Allah’s Light is exclusively His own and that all other lights emanate from His Light, …

(Abu Althana Shihabuddin Mahmud al-Alusi.  'Ruh al-Ma'ani fi Tafsir al-Quran al-Azim wa al-Sabu al- 
  Mathānī').

Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth and that from Him alone is the luminosity of  eternalness.

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  'Purani Tehriren'.  1878, 1879, Anwar Ahmadiyya Press, 1899.  'Ruhani Khazain: ii'.  19).

The latent light that removes all the metaphoric darkness only comes from Allah and it comes  to those who completely reduce their own selves to naught, …

(Ahmad.  Address to Jalsa Salana, 29 December 1904.  'Malfuzat: iv'.  213).

Surely this light is only descended on the heart which has been burnished with the fire of self- effacement.

(Ahmad.  'Al Huda'.  Ziya al-Islam, 1902.  'R. K: xviii'.  308).

The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST20020705-UR.pdf
